title: SGLang NPU dependency conflict — install with --no-deps to preserve NPU torch
problem_description: SGLang's standard pip distribution enforces torch==2.11.0 CUDA dependency which destroys the torch 2.6.0+cpu + torch_npu 2.6.0.post5 setup required for Ascend NPU. Standard pip install sglang overwrites NPU torch with CUDA torch, causing import failures and CUDA fallback markers. This conflict occurs on every SGLang-on-Ascend-NPU deployment because sglang's setup.py/setup.cfg does not yet have an ascend extra that relaxes the torch constraint.
rough_fix_approach: Step 1: Install 7 missing CANN-toolkit transitive Python deps (decorator scipy attrs cloudpickle ml-dtypes tornado absl-py) from Tsinghua mirror. Step 2: Install sglang with --no-deps to make it importable without overwriting torch. Step 3: Selectively install only sglang's non-CUDA runtime dependencies (aiohttp, fastapi, requests, etc.) that don't conflict with torch_npu. Use pip list before and after to verify torch+npu imports survive the sglang install.

involved_code_files.role: Migration entry script — import preflight probes torch, torch_npu, tbe, te, sglang; exits early on import failure before launch command

reasoning: This conflict is deterministic and universal for any SGLang deployment on Ascend NPU. The torch version mismatch between NPU ecosystems (torch+cpu with torch_npu) and SGLang's CUDA torch constraint will trigger on every migration. The --no-deps + selective reinstall pattern is non-obvious and requires awareness of which sglang sub-dependencies are safe (aiohttp, fastapi, requests) vs dangerous (torch, triton, cuda-python). The pattern also failed silently at first — the import preflight caught missing tbe/te deps before the torch conflict became visible, requiring iterative diagnosis. Highly actionable with step-by-step instructions.
